attempt_1_spatial_McCormick_S_branching.comment: Removed binary expansion but bound remained too weak; not enough for certification.

attempt_2_Gini_cap_frontier_full_arc_no_.comment: This is the region containing the known optimum G≈0.2176; certification remained hard.

attempt_3_Gini_cap_route_oracle_master.comment: The master is much smaller, but naive no-good lazy cuts are too weak because many solutions share the same inventory target but violate load-feasible route duration. It needs lifted resource-route cuts before it is competitive.

main_conclusion: The Gini-cap frontier reformulation is the best modeling improvement found: it removes the G*Y binary expansion and solves several Pareto frontier points in seconds. However, on the uploaded V12_M1_average instance it still did not certify the global optimum faster than the strengthened MILP because the low-Gini region remained difficult.
